item.text: {"outcome":"fail","confidence":"Firm","findings":[{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset tokens are generated with a non-cryptographic PRNG","severity":"Medium","path":"src/reset.js","line":6,"quote":"const token = `${Date.now().toString(36)}${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;"},{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Successful reset-token verification does not consume the token","severity":"Medium","path":"src/reset.js","line":19,"quote":"return expected.length === actual.length && timingSafeEqual(expected, actual);"}],"reasoning":"Password hashing uses bcrypt at cost 12 and bcrypt.compare. The reset-token digest comparison is length-checked and constant-time. The reset limiter is registered before the reset route, and the reset response is generic. However, reset tokens use Math.random rather than a CSPRNG, and verification only checks record.used without setting it, so a verified token remains reusable until another component changes the record."}
